title: Berlin U-Bahn Etiquette: Quick Summary
items: **Let people off before you get on.** Wait at the sides of the doors, then board and move into the carriage. **Erst aussteigen lassen, dann einsteigen.**
**Stand on the right of the escalator and leave the left clear.** At interchanges such as Alexanderplatz and Friedrichstraße, keep your suitcase on the right with you. **Rechts stehen, links gehen.**
**Many doors only open when you press the button.** Wait for it to light up, then push. The train does not open every door for you automatically.
**Leave marked priority seats for passengers who need them.** Keep bags off seats, take your backpack off in a crowded car, and use headphones for sound.
**Keep food and drinks closed on board.** Current VBB conditions prohibit boarding with open food or an open drink and consuming them during the journey.
**A bike normally needs its own ticket.** Use the marked area, give wheelchairs and prams priority, and wait for another train if it is full. Current guidance has no blanket weekday rush-hour ban.
**Always carry a valid ticket and validate it when required.** Open platforms have no barriers, but inspectors check trains and can issue a €60 EBE notice.
